name: MGM-3A "Sedna"
description: The Sedna introduces microwave subsurface disaggregation — directing focused 2.45 GHz energy into the regolith matrix to rapidly volatilize interstitial ices and shatter silicate grain bonds. The liberated slurry of fragments and sublimated gas is captured by a wide-aperture suction manifold. The technique eliminates percussion reaction forces almost entirely, greatly reducing anchor requirements. Throughput scales with subsurface ice and void fraction; effective on both dry silicates and moderately icy regolith.
